creator: mfechner@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Max from comment #6)
Gitlab uses a system account called git which has its home on /usr/local/git (maybe check here the file /usr/ports/UIDs).
You always authenticate against gitlab installation using the user git and your public ssh key. You must upload your public key on the gitlab webinterface for your gitlab user, which is not git, but maybe max.

I have the feeling that you mix here the users a little bit.
Gitlab is using the system user git to run itself and also to control access to repositories if you use ssh to pull/push.

But the user git is just a system user and has nothing to do with the users you create inside gitlab. It is only required to execute gitlab.

Do not mix here standard unix account you use with the service account gitlab uses to run. The user git is a system account and you should not work with it as a unix account.
